title: rosa sepium var. graveolens
intro: Rosa sepium var. graveolens, also known as the fragrant dog rose, is a variety of rose bush native to Europe and western Asia, characterized by its strong fragrance and thornless canes. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing pain and inflammation, and preventing infections. The health benefits of Rosa sepium var. graveolens include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of various respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Rosa sepium var. graveolens, such as infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
